answers.value: We receive cardboard packaging primarily from UK distributors and wholesalers who supply our raw materials. Approximately 60% comes from three main UK distributors, with the remaining 40% sourced directly from manufacturers in the Midlands region.

UK packaging waste

Total tonnage of the packaging waste category sent to other sites for recycling
Total contaminants from recycling the packaging waste category
Total process loss from recycling the packaging waste category
What is the key plant and equipment used to recycle this packaging waste category?
answers.value: We use shredding equipment, magnetic separators for ferrous metals, and eddy current separators for non-ferrous materials. Aluminium cans are densified using compactors before melting. Our de-coating line removes lacquers and printing before the material enters the furnace for remelting into ingots.

definition.name: Demo for pEPR - Extended Producer Responsibilities: register as a packaging waste reprocessor (EA)

pages.title: Form guidance

components.content:
This form is used to apply for registration with the Environment Agency (EA) as a reprocessor of packaging waste.

**You must submit this form once for each packaging waste category you intend to register.**

The form will take around 60 minutes to complete. It is recommended to have the following information to hand before starting to fill in the form.

## What you'll need to complete this form
Below is a list of the information you'll need in order to submit the form:

* organisation ID number
* site address details
* grid reference for the site
* address for service of notices
* permit or waste exemption details
* waste carrier, broker or dealer registration number
* maximum capacity for recycling at the site
* a list of the types of supplier you use
* input tonnages for calendar year 2024
* output tonnages for calendar year 2024
* a list of key plant and equipment at the site
* Packaging Exporter Sampling and Inspection Plan – Part 1: Registration - template available on gov.uk [here](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/extended-producer-responsibility-for-packaging-templates-for-sampling-and-inspection-plans)


**You cannot save and return to the form, it must be completed within one sitting.**

pages.title: Do you have an Organisation ID number?

components.shortDescription: Do you have an org ID?

pages.title: Apply for an Organisation ID

components.content:
To be able to register as a reprocessor of packaging waste you must first apply for an Organisation ID (Org ID).

You can apply for an Org ID with the Environment Agency by using this form: https://submit-form-to-defra.service.gov.uk/form/preview/draft/extended-producer-responsibilities-provide-your-organisation-details-ea

pages.title: Packaging waste the site has a permit or exemption to accept and recycle

components.content:
You need to tell us all the materials the site has authorisation to accept and recycle. 

The 'R' code is the classification of the reprocessing activity. These are in Annex 2 of the [Waste Framework Directive.](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A02008L0098-20240218)

components.content:
Regulators need to know the mix of waste types and raw materials used in your recycling process.

Only include the tonnage that relates to the packaging waste category you are registering.

components.content: Only include the tonnage that relates to the recycling process for the packaging waste category you are registering.

components.name: TTBOhH

components.title: Total contaminants from recycling the packaging waste category
components.name: zWAYkP
components.hint: Contaminants removed from the material during recycling, including non-recyclable components or impurities

components.title: Total process loss from recycling the packaging waste category
components.name: sfMNQP
components.hint: For example, loss through material degradation or waste

components.content: Only include the tonnage that relates to the packaging waste category you are registering.

pages.title: Products made from recycling for 2024

components.content: Only include the tonnage that relates to the recycling process for the packaging waste category you are registering.

components.hint: Enter the name of the product, for example milk bottles, food trays or cardboard boxes

components.title: Tonnage of product
components.name: CShkze
components.hint: Enter whole tonnages

pages.title: What is the key plant and equipment used to recycle this packaging waste category?

components.title: What is the key plant and equipment used to recycle this packaging waste category?
components.name: mVuNew
components.hint: You only need to enter the names of the equipment - we'll ask you more detail about how you use the equipment in the sampling and inspection plan

pages.title: Sampling and inspection plan

components.content:
You need to supply a plan explaining how you determine the amount of UK packaging waste received for recycling at this site - this is also called a sampling and inspection plan (SIP). 

The template for the SIP is available on gov.uk here: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/extended-producer-responsibility-for-packaging-templates-for-sampling-and-inspection-plans

Use the template called 'Packaging Reprocessor Sampling and Inspection Plan - Part 1: Registration.'

components.title: Upload part 1 of your sampling and inspection plan
components.name: xddzIW
components.hint: You may also upload supporting documentation

pages.title: Approved person

components.content:
An 'approved person' is needed to take on tasks on behalf of the organisation. One must be added to:

* apply for accreditation
* submit and verify data returns

An approved person may delegate their function to another person (a delegate) at a later stage - subject to regulator approval. 

Contact the regulator if you wish to delegate the functions of the approved person or add an additional approved person. 

##  Approved person details

pages.title: Summary

components.content:
If you want to keep a record of your submitted answers please print this page.

By submitting this application you confirm the information you provided is accurate.

**You may face enforcement action if the data is inaccurate.**

main.AyMrzj: Environmental permit or waste management licence
main.HFOWKa: We receive cardboard packaging primarily from UK distributors and wholesalers who supply our raw materials. Approximately 60% comes from three main UK distributors, with the remaining 40% sourced directly from manufacturers in the Midlands region.
main.mVuNew: We use shredding equipment, magnetic separators for ferrous metals, and eddy current separators for non-ferrous materials. Aluminium cans are densified using compactors before melting. Our de-coating line removes lacquers and printing before the material enters the furnace for remelting into ingots.
